content:
corporation
3. (1) The person for the time being discharging the duties of
Chief Commissioner of the Association, and her successors in
office, shall be a body corporate, under the name of the “Chief
Commissioner, Girl Guides Association of Malaysia”, in this Act
called the “corporation”.
6
Laws of Malaysia
ACT 456
(2) The corporation may enter into contracts and may sue and
be sued in its said name and shall have perpetual succession and
a corporate seal, and the said seal may from time to time be
broken, changed, altered and made anew as to the corporation
deems fit; and until a seal is provided under this section a stamp,
bearing the inscription “Chief Commissioner, Girl Guides Association
of Malaysia”, may be used as the corporate seal.
(3) The corporation may acquire, purchase, take, hold and enjoy
movable and immovable property of every description, and may
sell, convey, assign, surrender and yield up, charge, mortgage,
demise, reassign, transfer or otherwise dispose of, or deal with,
any movable or immovable property vested in the said corporation
upon such terms as to the corporation deems fit.
Execution of documents
4.
All deeds, documents and other instruments requiring the seal
of the corporation shall be sealed with the seal of the corporation
in the presence of the person for the time being discharging the
duties of Chief Commissioner of the Association, who shall sign
every such deed, document or other instrument to which the corporate
seal is affixed, and such signing shall be sufficient evidence that
the said seal was duly and properly affixed and that the same is
the lawful seal of the corporation.
Appointment
5.
A notification in the Gazette of the appointment of any person
to hold, or act in, the office of Chief Commissioner of the Association
shall be conclusive evidence that such person was duly so appointed.
Restriction on sale of badges, etc., of the Association
6.
No person, other than the Association, shall sell or expose for
sale any badge, token or emblem specifically adopted for use by
a Guide under the Rules of the Association, except that such
badges, tokens or emblems may be sold or exposed for sale by a
person authorized in writing so to do by Chief Commissioner of
the Association.
7
Girl Guides
Restriction on use of badges
7.
No person, not being a Guide, shall publicly wear, carry or
bear in such a manner as to convey the impression that such person
is a Guide any badge, token or emblem specifically adopted for
use by a Guide under the Rules of the Association.
Possession of badges
8.
No person shall without lawful authority or excuse have in his
possession—
(a) any device which so closely resembles any badge, token
or emblem specifically adopted for use by a Guide under
the Rules of the Association as to lead to the belief that
the device in question is such badge, token or emblem;
(b) any badge, token or emblem containing any words or
characters so closely resembling any words or characters
ordinarily used to describe any Guide as to be calculated
to deceive or mislead; or
(c) any badge, token or emblem bearing the words “Girl
Guide” or “Girl Guides”.
Wrongful exercise of authority by a Guide
9.
No Guide shall seek or attempt by virtue of her wearing,
carrying or bearing any badge, token or emblem of the Association,
or badge, token or emblem purporting to be such, to enforce or
exercise authority otherwise than in accordance with and as authorized
by the Rules of the Association.
False claims of connection
10.
No person shall form, organize or work in connection with,
or be concerned in forming, organizing or working in connection
with, any corps or body which without due authority claims or
purports to be “Girl Guides” or otherwise to be connected with the
Association.
8
Laws of Malaysia
ACT 456
Prohibition of unauthorized organizations

Prohibition of unauthorized organizations
11. (1) No person shall, without the consent of the Yang di-
Pertuan Agong, form or work in connection with or be a member
of any organization other than the Association which carries on
or is intended to carry on any work of a similar nature to that
carried on by the Association.
(2) Subsection (1) shall apply although such organization is, or
is contained within, a Society registered or exempted under the
Societies Act 1966 [Act 335].
Penalty

Penalty
12. Any person contravening any of the provisions of this Act
shall be liable to a fine of five hundred ringgit.
9
Girl Guides
